danger_signals.visual_form: Barker Bill, Trixie, or parrot positioned near or overlapping with interactive objects on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Risk zone—shooting these characters costs a life; signals constraint and hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Window visually blocked or marked as closed in Window Pains stage
danger_signals.meaning: Impassable barrier; shooting blocked objects results in miss and life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Objects descending rapidly in Window Pains stage
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure; objects disappearing = failed target and life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Balloons flying away, plates approaching floor, objects falling off screen
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent failure state; spatial positioning communicates urgency

safety_signals.visual_form: Diamond object appearing on screen during stages
safety_signals.meaning: Extra life currency; safe to shoot, rewards player with insurance against mistakes

safety_signals.visual_form: Unblocked window visually distinguishable from closed ones
safety_signals.meaning: Safe shooting corridor; indicates valid targets can be hit

safety_signals.visual_form: Trixie tosses coins explicitly; temporal presentation signal
safety_signals.meaning: Designated target moment; safe to shoot when actively presented

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Barker Bill, Trixie, parrot—carnival performer avatars
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Carnival/fair setting; game show aesthetic with objects as props

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Duck Hunt dog cameo in Balloon Saloon mode
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dog is protected entity (avoid shooting); visual callback to Duck Hunt paradigm
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Balloons + dog presence = Duck Hunt thematic borrowing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Slower object movement, fewer blocking elements, fewer character obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Targets linger longer on screen; parrot and characters appear less frequently

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Objects move faster, more windows blocked, increased character presence on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Trixie introduces coins as bonus target; Bill's Thrills stage adds small, fast-moving objects

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen transitions to slot machine interface; diamonds previously collected influence visual slot lines
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player pulls trigger to stop reels; visual complexity of winning lines increases with diamond count

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bill's Thrills stage: tiny eggs and tomatoes, fastest object speeds, parrot omnipresent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Highest point values; maximum difficulty; objects thrown at extreme heights requiring anticipatory aim

environmental_storytelling.example: Four game modes (Balloon Saloon, Flying Saucers, Window Pains, Fun Follies) presented as themed carnival attractions; setting communicates low-stakes fun vs. arcade competition
Barker Bill as showman, Trixie as assistant, parrot as sidekick—visual hierarchy establishes game-show aesthetic distinct from combat/action paradigm
Balloons = flight/escape, plates = fragility, coins/diamonds = reward, eggs/tomatoes = messy consequences—visual object choice reinforces carnival tone
Window Pains introduces architectural constraint (windows); Fun Follies adds gambling mechanic (slot machine)—visual environment shifts signal rule changes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Tosses eggs and tomatoes high up; player must intercept before objects land on Trixie; position indicates threat/obstacle
Walks across screen presenting coins; occasional movement signals variable timing challenge; player must avoid hitting her
Appears throughout stages as protected entity; hitting parrot costs a life; visual marker for collision avoidance
Flies across Balloon Saloon stage; protected entity; signals crossover and establishes safe-zone logic
Motion trajectory communicates point value and time pressure; proximity to screen edge signals imminent disappearance

notes: Barker Bill's Trick Shooting is a light gun carnival simulator with minimal narrative complexity. World-state communication relies entirely on visual object placement, character positioning, and stage-specific environmental layouts (windows, slot machine). Danger is signaled through character proximity and object trajectory rather than explicit visual warnings. The game lacks faction conflict, political narrative, or world-state text; all state information is conveyed through object affordances and spatial composition. Paradigm is 'other' (carnival game emulation) rather than traditional action/RPG/strategy. Data quality is T3 qualitative due to Wikipedia source limitations.
